sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE NG125; last updated 2020-08-19; last reviewed 2023-05-31
sources.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
sources.clinical_setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for postoperative wound-care and surgical-site-infection documentation; partial for traumatic wounds, suture removal, and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — information, wound care, and infection recognition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

exact_sections.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.8
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.9–1.4.11 and surgical-site-infection definition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

archive.title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
archive.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — information, wound care, and infection recognition
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.8
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.9–1.4.11 and surgical-site-infection definition

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people with suspected or diagnosed thyroid disease, subject to recommendation-specific age and disease qualifiers.
sources.clinical_setting: Assessment, investigation, treatment discussion, monitoring, and follow-up of thyroid disease.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected thyroid-testing, thyroid-enlargement warning, hypothyroidism-monitoring, and hyperthyroidism-monitoring documentation. Symptom diagnosis, medicine selection or dose, and UAE pathways remain clinician responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — information and thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.2 — suspected dysfunction testing and antibodies
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician suspicion context, mood or anxiety context, TSH, FT4, FT3, biotin history, repeat testing when symptoms change, and TPO antibody testing in recommendation-qualified cases.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.6 — follow-up and monitoring of primary hypothyroidism
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH and FT4 monitoring, persistent-symptom review, and clinician-led follow-up for confirmed primary hypothyroidism without mapping a dose or autonomous treatment change.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.5 — follow-up and monitoring of hyperthyroidism
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.8 — thyroid enlargement with normal thyroid function
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of palpable thyroid enlargement or focal nodularity, recommendation-qualified ultrasound, compression symptoms, and clinician-led specialist assessment.
